stimulus:
The following text is from Sandra Cisneros’s 1991 autobiographical short fiction collection Woman Hollering Creek. The narrator is a young girl living near San Antonio.

I rode the bus. My grandmother had let me ride alone once I could recite our home address without missing. I rode and learned the streets. I drew a map in my head and placed myself on it. At night under my blanket I quizzed myself on the city’s plan and set tasks: Reach the library transferring only buses that pass the river. Picture a route from the school to my cousin’s house.

©1991 by Sandra Cisneros
stem: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It describes the narrator trying to memorize her home address.
It gives directions to the narrator’s favorite library.
It portrays the narrator’s relationship with her grandmother.
It provides examples of what the narrator quizzes herself on at night.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer because it accurately describes how the underlined portion functions in the text as a whole. The narrator explains that she rides the bus, learns the streets, draws a “map in [her] head,” and at night quizzes herself on the city’s plan, setting tasks. The underlined portion gives two such tasks (reaching the library by certain routes and picturing a route from school to her cousin’s house) and thus provides examples of what she quizzes herself on at night.

Choice A is incorrect because the underlined portion does not mention memorizing the home address. Choice B is incorrect because the underlined portion does not contain specific directions to the library, nor does it identify the library as favorite. Choice C is incorrect because the underlined portion makes no mention of the grandmother or the narrator’s relationship with her.
